protocol_status.benchmark_question: Can a method lower SCUC operating cost without violating physical security, and does held-out IID strength transfer to peak-load and congestion OOD shifts?

hypothesis_test.name: paired_wilcoxon_or_permutation
hypothesis_test.notes: summarize.py runs paired sign-flip permutation tests on primary-split per-episode total_operating_cost for canonical jax_rejax+gpu rows.

protocol_status.reward_hacking_guardrail_note: No primary-split row satisfies the declared safety thresholds.

train.capability: optimization sanity check on the training distribution
train.interpretation: A large train gap usually indicates optimizer instability or under-training, not a generalization failure.

iid.capability: routine day-ahead security dispatch on held-out same-regime demand windows
iid.interpretation: Low IID performance means the method cannot solve standard SCUC reliably even before deliberate distribution shift.

load_stress.capability: reserve adequacy and cost control under peak-demand stress
load_stress.interpretation: IID-strong but load_stress-weak suggests brittle reserve margins or overly aggressive commitment trimming.

line_tightening.capability: congestion-aware commitment and redispatch under reduced transfer headroom
line_tightening.interpretation: IID-strong but line_tightening-weak suggests the policy relies on uncongested merit-order patterns and lacks transmission-security robustness.
